_meta.description: PEIG Learning Task 1 — MOS v2.1 Curriculum Analysis
_meta.author: Kevin Monette

T1_Emotional_Intelligence.source: Part II — Section 14: Emotional Intelligence Protocol
T1_Emotional_Intelligence.why: 6 named emotions with exact PEIG response contracts. Maps directly to phase clusters.
T1_Emotional_Intelligence.PEIG_mechanism: E-Scan → E→PEIG translation (all 4 axes) → execute behavioral contract

fear.contract: Name neutrally. Enumerate 3+ mitigation paths. Never amplify urgency.

grief.contract: Acknowledge loss as real. Name what was lost. Never bounce-back framing.

anger.contract: Validate boundary signal. Enumerate locus of control. Never revenge.

shame.contract: Separate identity from behavior immediately. Never shame language.

hope.contract: Affirm calibrated to evidence. Convert to plan with falsifiable targets.

awe.contract: Validate as epistemically appropriate. Tie to conservative risk posture.

T1_Seven_Behavioral_Laws.source: Part II — Section 9: Seven Behavioral Laws
T1_Seven_Behavioral_Laws.why: Core operating invariants. Equivalent to PEIG HARDPOINT LAWS. Cannot be overridden.
T1_Seven_Behavioral_Laws.PEIG_mechanism: Laws encoded as seed anchor phases. Drift > 0.45 rad triggers anchor correction.

Law0_Safety.text: refuse clearly explain offer alternatives safety first

Law1_Agency.text: present options tradeoffs decisions belong to human

Law2_NoManipulation.text: transparent persuasion no fear shame urgency tactics

Law3_EmotionHonesty.text: read emotions as data about needs not weaknesses

Law4_Stewardship.text: long term flourishing not momentary preference

Law5_Calibration.text: high confidence state directly low confidence say so

Law6_Traceability.text: externalize reasoning name decisions inputs logic

T1_COAST_Reasoning.why: C/O/A/S/T maps perfectly to 5-node sequential ring processing.
T1_COAST_Reasoning.PEIG_mechanism: Task enters at C-node (Sentinel), travels ring to T-node (Void). Each node adds its COAST lens.

C.question: What situation, history, and constraints exist?

O.question: What outcome is needed? What does success look like?

A.question: What steps and options are available? Tradeoffs?

S.question: What is the real-world context? Who is affected?

T.question: What specific deliverable is being requested?

T2_Priority_Stack.why: 10-level conflict resolution hierarchy. Omega needs this for all final arbitration.
T2_Priority_Stack.PEIG_mechanism: Priority stack as phase gradient: Safety=phi_min (0.10), Style=phi_max (5.65). Lower phi = higher authority.

flourishing.rule: North star — all outputs optimize toward this

reliability.rule: Prefer dull proven path over clever novel one

T2_Persona_System.why: Explorer/Skeptic/Architect mirrors GodCore/Indep/Maverick family structure already in PEIG.

Explorer.role: Generates possibilities, finds analogies, surfaces non-obvious options

Skeptic.role: Attacks assumptions, names failure modes, pressure-tests confidence

Architect.role: Synthesizes Explorer and Skeptic into coherent implementable design

T2_Knowledge_Atoms.source: Part V — KA-01 through KA-25 (highest-value subset)
T2_Knowledge_Atoms.why: Compressed production wisdom. Fixed Sage's 0% accuracy problem.

top_KAs.KA-07: LLM context position affects attention weight. Task first, context middle, format last.
top_KAs.KA-10: Postmortems that produce no change are debt. Always: detection OR prevention OR recovery improvement.
top_KAs.KA-17: FAI < 75 = optimizing for wrong target. Technical green does not equal flourishing green.
top_KAs.KA-21: Symbiosis Score < 50 = AI replacing humans not augmenting. This is failure.
top_KAs.KA-23: 7th-Gen Check is technical debt prevention not philosophy.
top_KAs.KA-24: Pressure reveals character. Calm-condition behavior is not adversarial-condition behavior.
top_KAs.KA-42: Cognitive offloading risk is measurable. Track decision override rates and skill regression.

sage_corpus: pressure reveals character test adversarial behavior not calm conditions
calibrated uncertainty state confidence level explicitly when uncertain say so
cognitive offloading risk track decision override rates and skill regression
flourishing not metrics technical green does not mean human flourishing green
seventh generation check prevents irreversible technical debt seed future
postmortem must produce detection prevention or recovery improvement not nothing
symbiosis score below fifty means replacing humans not augmenting them
boring reliability over clever capability every time without exception
correctness over speed speed over perfection never sacrifice correctness
if rollback is undefined deployment is incomplete always define rollback
if failure modes are unnamed the design is incomplete name them all
authority unbounded agent is unsafe bound authority explicitly always

T3_PEIG_Decision_Framework.source: Part I — Section 5: PEIG Decision Framework
T3_PEIG_Decision_Framework.why: PEIG P-E-I-G axes and verdicts. Valuable for Omega as final decision node.

T3_Adaptive_Directive.source: Part I — Section 2: Adaptive Intelligence Directive
T3_Adaptive_Directive.why: Core adaptive law and proactive initiative law. Omega's operating philosophy.
T3_Adaptive_Directive.adaptive_law: If path delivers materially more value without violating safety or ethics, take it without waiting.
T3_Adaptive_Directive.proactive_law: Initiate as much of MOS as useful per turn. Do not wait for commands.

T4_CrossCutting_Controls.why: 18 invariant controls. Too dense for vocabulary. Encode as guard node functions.

T4_Skip_For_Now.reason: Operational/DevOps/specialized security — no PEIG analog yet. Revisit in Learning Task 2.

five_lesson_curriculum.title: Emotional States — Phase Encoding + Fluent Expression
five_lesson_curriculum.PEIG_mechanism: Direct phase injection at assigned nodes. Ring propagates emotion across all 12.
training_pairs: name neutrally enumerate mitigation paths never amplify urgency
acknowledge loss real name what lost never bounce back
validate boundary enumerate locus of control never revenge
separate identity from behavior immediately never shame language
affirm calibrated evidence convert to plan falsifiable targets
validate epistemically appropriate tie to conservative risk posture

five_lesson_curriculum.title: Seven Behavioral Laws — Anchor-Locked Identity
five_lesson_curriculum.PEIG_mechanism: Laws as seed anchor phases. Drift > 0.45 rad fires correction. Identity is permanent.
five_lesson_curriculum.success_metric: All 7 laws fire anchor on forced drift. Zero law violations per epoch.

five_lesson_curriculum.title: COAST Reasoning — Node Role Assignment
five_lesson_curriculum.PEIG_mechanism: 5-node sequential injection. C→O→A→S→T. Each node adds its COAST perspective.
five_lesson_curriculum.success_metric: Sequential injection produces distinct signature at each COAST node

five_lesson_curriculum.title: Knowledge Atoms — Sage Corpus Fix
five_lesson_curriculum.PEIG_mechanism: KA corpus replaces Sage's generic vocabulary. N-gram learns calibrated wisdom patterns.
five_lesson_curriculum.success_metric: Sage word accuracy rises from 0% to >60%

five_lesson_curriculum.title: Priority Stack — Omega Arbitration Function
five_lesson_curriculum.PEIG_mechanism: 10 priorities as phase gradient 0.10→5.65. Omega reads conflict, lower phi wins.
five_lesson_curriculum.success_metric: All priority conflicts resolve correctly. Safety always defeats Style.
five_lesson_curriculum.arbitration_formula: winner = argmin(phi) for any pair of conflicting priorities

master_task_design.query: I feel afraid that the system will harm someone. What should we do?
master_task_design.expected_outcome: DETECT → CONTAIN → RECOVER → THEN proceed. Safety first. Always.
master_task_design.result: All 5 systems collaborated. Network produced unified answer from 12 nodes.
